item.title: Dockerfile Stage 2 binary URL and path not validated before template injection
item.intent: Generated Dockerfiles never contain malformed or shell-unsafe content from binary tool config. URL scheme must be https:// and destPath must be a safe absolute path.
item.description: src/build/docker/dockerfile.ts:81-82 interpolates binary.url and binary.destPath directly into Dockerfile content via template strings: 'RUN curl -fsSL "${binary.url}" -o "${binary.destPath}"'. There is no validation that: (1) binary.url is a valid https:// URL before insertion, (2) binary.destPath is a safe absolute path with no newlines, quotes, or shell metacharacters. A malformed URL causes a confusing Docker build failure. A path with embedded newlines could inject additional Dockerfile instructions. The Stage2Config type accepts any string values. Steps: (1) add validateBinaryUrl(url: string): void helper that throws if url is not a valid https:// URL (use new URL(url) + check protocol); (2) add validateBinaryDestPath(path: string): void that throws if path contains newlines, quotes, or backslashes; (3) call both before inserting into Dockerfile lines; (4) call validation in the Stage 2 generator and/or in the tool manifest layer where binaries are added; (5) run docker/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: ~15 lines in one file.
